participants.extracted: Adults aged 18–55 years with relapsing–remitting multiple sclerosis (RRMS), baseline EDSS 0–5.0, and evidence of recent disease activity (at least one clinically documented relapse within 12 months before randomization, or at least one gadolinium-enhancing brain MRI lesion within 6 weeks before randomization). Patients with progressive forms of MS or other major exclusions were not eligible. The MRI cohort analyzed here was a subset of the overall trial population and included 540 randomized participants who had any MRI data: 180 assigned to placebo, 176 to delayed-release dimethyl fumarate 240 mg twice daily, and 184 to delayed-release dimethyl fumarate 240 mg three times daily.

interventions.extracted: Delayed-release dimethyl fumarate (DMF; BG-12, Tecfidera) administered orally at 240 mg twice daily or 240 mg three times daily; in the DEFINE trial these active treatment arms were compared with placebo in adults with relapsing-remitting multiple sclerosis.

outcomes.extracted: Primary outcome: proportion of patients who experienced an MS relapse by 2 years. Secondary MRI outcomes: number of new or enlarging T2-hyperintense lesions on brain MRI at 2 years; number of gadolinium-enhancing (Gd+) lesions on brain MRI at 2 years. Tertiary outcomes: number of new or enlarging T2-hyperintense lesions at 1 year; number of Gd+ lesions at 1 year; number of new non-enhancing T1-hypointense lesions at 1 year and 2 years; volume of all T2-hyperintense lesions at 6 months, 1 year, and 2 years; volume of Gd+ lesions at 6 months, 1 year, and 2 years; volume of non-enhancing T1-hypointense lesions at 6 months, 1 year, and 2 years; and brain atrophy measured as percentage brain volume change (PBVC) at 2 years, with a pre-specified analysis of PBVC from 6 months to 2 years. Prespecified subgroup MRI analyses also assessed numbers of new or enlarging T2-hyperintense lesions and Gd+ lesions at 2 years across baseline demographic and disease-characteristic strata.
